CBP Officers Seize 45 Pounds of Methamphetamine at Laredo Port of Entry

April 18, 2026

LAREDO, Texas – U.S. Customs and Border Protection officers at the Laredo Port of Entry seized approximately 45 pounds of methamphetamine on Friday after a passenger vehicle underwent secondary inspection.

Officers referred a 34-year-old U.S. citizen driving a 2019 Chevrolet Silverado to secondary inspection. A CBP narcotics detector dog alerted to the vehicle, and a subsequent physical examination revealed 22 packages of methamphetamine concealed inside the vehicle's door panels. The estimated street value of the seized narcotics is approximately $90,000.

CBP officers seized the narcotics and turned the driver over to Homeland Security Investigations (HSI). The driver was arrested and faces federal drug trafficking charges.

CBP's mission is to keep terrorists and their weapons out of the U.S. while facilitating lawful international travel and trade.
